topic: Composio Agent Orchestrator: Parallel Coding Agents for CI and PR Reviews

key_points: AO vs T3 Code vs OpenAI Symphony vs Cmux competitive landscape discussion
AO focuses on full lifecycle PR autonomy with automatic CI fix handling
Symphony uses Elixir OTP fault-tolerance and integrates with Linear
T3 Code prioritizes interactive per-edit review with JSON-RPC
Hands-on comparison of four leading agent orchestration tools
AO defaults to 5 concurrent agents (configurable), Symphony defaults to 10
AO requires Node 20+, pnpm, tmux, git 2.25+
Symphony requires Elixir, PostgreSQL, Linear workspace, OpenAI key
AO is production-ready and backed by Composio; Symphony is an engineering preview
OpenAI Symphony is a SPEC.md-based open-source spec for Codex orchestration
Polls Linear boards, auto-claims tickets, spawns Codex agents
Uses Erlang/OTP supervision trees for process-level fault tolerance
Versioned workflow configuration in WORKFLOW.md
Roundup of 9 open-source agent orchestrators for AI coding in 2026
Covers feature comparison, setup complexity, agent-agnosticism
AO stands out for autonomous PR lifecycle management
Various tools cater to different levels of human-in-the-loop involvement
Composio open-sourced Agent Orchestrator in February 2026
Replaces ReAct loops with a dual-layered Planner/Executor architecture
Manages 100+ APIs and eliminates tool noise in production AI
Just-in-Time context management routes only relevant tool definitions

stats.fact: Composio Agent Orchestrator has 4.9k GitHub stars since open-sourcing in February 2026
The system comprises 40,000 lines of TypeScript, 17 plugins, and 3,288 tests — built in 8 days, mostly by the agents it orchestrates
Default concurrency is 5 parallel agents per project (configurable up to 30+)
Agent Orchestrator supports 8 plugin slots: runtime, agent, workspace, tracker, SCM, notifier, terminal, lifecycle
CI failure detection and routing back to the agent session occurs within approximately 30 seconds via lifecycle polling
Agent Orchestrator is agent-agnostic (Claude Code, Codex, Aider), runtime-agnostic (tmux, Docker), and tracker-agnostic (GitHub, Linear)

angles: How Composio built its own orchestrator using the orchestrator itself (self-bootstrapping story)
Full PR lifecycle autonomy: from task to merged PR without human intervention
Comparison with OpenAI Symphony: open-source spec vs opinionated framework
The shift from ReAct loops to stateful, dual-layer orchestration in production AI
Practical setup guide: from zero to running 5 parallel coding agents in minutes
Real-world use case: how teams offload CI fix loops and PR review cycles to AI agents
Plugin ecosystem: extending Agent Orchestrator for custom CI systems and project trackers
